reason: RFID tags are implanted/attached to animals for identification and tracking; POS/PPS are not tags, GPS is not a tag on a database of movements read by readers.
"Chip" is the common nickname for an integrated circuit (many transistors on one silicon die).
Printer is an output device, not storage; HDD, USB drive, DVD drive all store data.
Hardware is by definition the physical, touchable parts of a computer.
A keyboard feeds data into the computer, so it is an input device.
The CPU is called the brains of the computer and performs processing.
During boot the BIOS runs POST and then searches the boot devices for the OS/system files.
The monitor/screen is the output device that displays what the computer is doing; the printer outputs to paper, not a live view.
A scanner captures images into the computer (input); speaker and printer are output, CD is media.
Barcodes on retail items are read by a laser (barcode) scanner; a wand is a contact reader, OCR reads characters, MICR reads magnetic ink on cheques.
A hard disk is a physical storage component, i.e. computer hardware.
Computer-manipulated surgical devices are robotics (robot-assisted surgery).
A modem modulates digital signals to analog for telephone lines and demodulates on receipt; the other options are nonsense distractors.
Laptops are the portable computers used by travelers.

Among the given options DPI (dots/pixels per inch) is the resolution measure; Hz is refresh rate and inches is screen size. Strictly, monitor resolution is usually quoted in pixels (e.g. 1920x1080), so the item is loosely worded.
CD-ROM is read by laser from pits/lands on a reflective layer: an optical disk, not magnetic or magneto-optical.
BIOS = Basic Input/Output System.
The motherboard hosts the CPU socket and RAM slots.
LCD = Liquid Crystal Display.
The cursor (pointer/insertion point) indicates the current location on screen; the mouse is the device that moves it.
